sections.paragraph:
δυσωδία, ας, ἡ (ὄζω ‘to emit an odor’; Aristot. et al.; Diod S 14, 71, 2; Longus 4, 17, 2; schol. on Nicander, Ther. 308; SEG 8, 621; Is 34:3 Sym.; Philo, Mos. 1, 100; 2, 262; TestBenj 8:3; TestJob [pl. 34:4] AcThom 55 [Aa II/2, 171, 17]) ‘a strong offensive odor, stench’, then also that which causes the stench, filth (Anna Comn., Alex. 13, 10 ed. Reiff. II 205, 10) ApcPt 11, 26. Fig. filth of erroneous teaching IEph 17:1.—DELG s.v. ὄζω.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Longus Longus , II ?a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

  1. pl. = plural

AcThom AcThom = Acts of Thomas—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 265
